title: Zygia sanguinea
taxonomy: Zygia sanguinea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Zygia, which is a group of flowering plants commonly found in tropical regions. The Fabaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants that includes legumes, such as beans, peas, and lentils. Zygia sanguinea is a part of the subfamily Faboideae, which contains many species of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant's classification is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular analysis, which have helped scientists understand its evolutionary relationships with other plants.
